sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports headaches, mild edema in the bilateral upper extremities, and left hand pain. He reports blurred vision and dizziness. The patient eats a lot of McDonald's and his favorite treat is a Big Mac with large fries. The patient does not follow a low sodium diet. The patient reports irregular metformin intake, approximately every other day. The patient takes tylenol.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Blood pressure is elevated. Heart rate is 88 bpm. Neurological exam is unremarkable.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient has uncontrolled hypertension, high blood pressure, and blurred vision.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: The doctor will monitor the patient's blood pressure three times a week. The doctor plans to increase the patient's metformin dosage to 1500 mg. The doctor instructs the patient to continue monitoring blood sugars. The doctor schedules a one-month follow-up appointment. The doctor will continue the patient's current medication regimen.
